comment_text.sn8.9:1.2: Koṇḍañña gained his nickname as he was the first person to realize the Dhamma after the Buddha (SN 56.11:14.3). His absence was due to his love of solitude, as expressed in his verses (Thag 15.1:0.4). Due to this, he features rarely in the early texts, but the Buddha once saw him meditating (Ud 7.6), and praised him as the senior mendicant (AN 1.188:1.1).
comment_text.sn8.9:3.1: Used more generally of early disciples at SN 16.5:5.3.
comment_text.sn8.9:3.4: Not the three consisting of body, mind, and attachments, for this is only found in later texts (Mnd 2:11.2). Perhaps the three consisting of ethics, view, and ending of defilements (AN 3.93:5.1). But since the absorptions are often called “blissful meditations” (SN 21.4:3.3) and “seclusion” (MN 64:9.2), it’s more satisfying to read the two phrases together.

root_text.sn8.9:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe.
root_text.sn8.9:1.2: Atha kho āyasmā aññāsikoṇḍañño sucirasseva yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavato pādesu sirasā nipatitvā bhagavato pādāni mukhena ca paricumbati, pāṇīhi ca parisambāhati, nāmañca sāveti:
root_text.sn8.9:1.5: “ayaṁ kho āyasmā aññāsikoṇḍañño sucirasseva yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavato pādesu sirasā nipatitvā bhagavato pādāni mukhena ca paricumbati, pāṇīhi ca parisambāhati, nāmañca sāveti:
root_text.sn8.9:1.7: Yannūnāhaṁ āyasmantaṁ aññāsikoṇḍaññaṁ bhagavato sammukhā sāruppāhi gāthāhi abhitthaveyyan”ti.
root_text.sn8.9:2.1: Atha kho āyasmā vaṅgīso uṭṭhāyāsanā ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā yena bhagavā tenañjaliṁ paṇāmetvā bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn8.9:2.4: Atha kho āyasmā vaṅgīso āyasmantaṁ aññāsikoṇḍaññaṁ bhagavato sammukhā sāruppāhi gāthāhi abhitthavi:

translation_text.sn8.9:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Rājagaha, in the Bamboo Grove, the squirrels’ feeding ground.
translation_text.sn8.9:1.2: Then Venerable Koṇḍañña Who Understood approached the Buddha after a very long absence. He bowed with his head at the Buddha’s feet, caressing them and covering them with kisses, and pronounced his name:
translation_text.sn8.9:1.3: “I am Koṇḍañña, Blessed One! I am Koṇḍañña, Holy One!”
translation_text.sn8.9:1.5: “This Venerable Koṇḍañña Who Understood has approached the Buddha after a very long absence. He bowed with his head at the Buddha’s feet, caressing them and covering them with kisses, and pronounced his name:
translation_text.sn8.9:1.6: ‘I am Koṇḍañña, Blessed One! I am Koṇḍañña, Holy One!’
translation_text.sn8.9:1.7: Why don’t I extoll him in the Buddha’s presence with fitting verses?”
translation_text.sn8.9:2.1: Then Venerable Vaṅgīsa got up from his seat, arranged his robe over one shoulder, raised his joined palms toward the Buddha, and said,
translation_text.sn8.9:2.2: “I feel inspired to speak, Blessed One! I feel inspired to speak, Holy One!”
translation_text.sn8.9:2.3: “Then speak as you feel inspired,” said the Buddha.
translation_text.sn8.9:2.4: Then Vaṅgīsa extolled Koṇḍañña in the Buddha’s presence with fitting verses:
translation_text.sn8.9:3.1: “The senior monk awakened after the Buddha,
translation_text.sn8.9:4.1: Whatever can be attained by a disciple
translation_text.sn8.9:5.1: With great power and the three knowledges,
translation_text.sn8.9:5.2: expert in encompassing the minds of others,
translation_text.sn8.9:5.3: Koṇḍañña, the heir to the Buddha,

reference_text.sn8.9:1.1: cck15.254, dr15.269, ms12S1_1356, msdiv217, ndp12.194, pts-vp-pli1ed1.194, pts-vp-pli2ed1.418, sya15.285
